title: Workspace Context Resolver + Authorization Enforcement
summary: Implements server-derived workspace context resolution and cross-workspace authorization enforcement. The workspace is resolved from operator identity and membership records. Client-supplied workspace_id is verified against membership before being accepted. Cross-workspace lookup and mutation are denied unless the operator holds an explicitly documented cross-workspace role (owner/grant_admin_global). Cross-tenant access is always denied. Fail-closed: operators with no memberships receive 403.

workspace_context_resolver.description: Derives the effective workspace_id from server-side auth payload and database membership records. Never trusts client-supplied workspace_id without verification.

resolution_modes.description: No operator in auth payload (legacy/admin-token mode). Resolves to the canonical demo workspace (id='default', tenant='demo'). Backward-compatible.
Operator has exactly one active workspace membership. Workspace is auto-resolved. No workspace_id required in request.
Operator supplies a workspace_id. Membership is verified against the database before the workspace is accepted.
Operator holds a cross-workspace role (owner/grant_admin_global) and supplies a workspace_id. Verified to belong to caller tenant. No membership check required. Explicitly documented — not silent.
Cross-workspace role operator in demo tenant without a workspace_id. Falls back to demo workspace. Backward-compatible for demo/synthetic mode.

fail_closed_cases: Operator has no active workspace memberships → 403 no_workspace_membership
Operator supplies workspace_id with no membership → 403 workspace_access_denied
Operator supplies workspace_id from another tenant → 403 workspace_not_found
Operator supplies workspace_id for inactive workspace → 403 workspace_inactive
Operator supplies empty or blank workspace_id → 400 invalid_workspace_id
Operator with multiple memberships and no workspace_id supplied → 400 workspace_id_required
Cross-workspace role without workspace_id in non-demo tenant → 400 workspace_id_required

authorization_enforcement.description: Enforces cross-workspace and cross-tenant access boundaries at the resource level.

rules.description: If resource_tenant_id differs from caller_tenant_id, access is always denied (403 cross_tenant_access_denied). No role or flag bypasses this.
If resource_workspace_id matches caller_workspace_id, access is allowed subject to role checks.
Resources with resource_workspace_id=NULL are treated as same-workspace (backward compat with pre-GL-224 rows). Always allowed.
Resource in a different workspace and cross_workspace_access=False → 403.
Write to resource in a different workspace and cross_workspace_access=False → 403.
cross_workspace_access=True (set only for owner/grant_admin_global roles) allows cross-workspace access. This is always explicit in the workspace context — never silent.
rules.note: Lookup and mutation are allowed. The workspace_member_role=workspace_readonly restriction still applies for mutations.

rules.description: workspace_member_role=workspace_readonly cannot perform mutations in any workspace.

notes: resolve_workspace_context is a pure helper in auth.py — it does not modify server.py routes.
check_workspace_resource_access is a pure enforcement function — callers pass resource metadata.
Demo/synthetic mode (no operator in payload) is fully backward-compatible.
Cross-workspace admin access is never silent: cross_workspace_access=True is always present in the returned context dict.
No new migration is required — this GL uses the workspace tables created by GL-224 migration 0011.
